name: ATX Indoor Golf Club
address: 5446 W US Hwy 290 Service Rd Unit 5, Austin, TX 78735
about: The ATX Indoor Golf Club provides a best-in-class, cutting edge indoor golf simulation experience utilizing industry-leading Trackman iO technology coupled with 4K UHD Laser Projection. The venue features two hitting bays designed with privacy in mind, offering a comfortable space with big screen TVs and flexible options for different player levels. Memberships and public tee times are available, providing a cost-efficient and accessible golfing environment that operates 24 hours a day, allowing members to play anytime.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to over 400 top-rated global courses with stunning 4K graphics
24/7 member access with flexible booking for solo practice or groups
Privacy-focused bays allowing a distraction-free environment
Comprehensive swing data and analysis via Trackman's AI-powered tools

food_and_drink.notes: Self-serve '19th Hole Pantry' with snacks; BYOB allowed for members and guests
No alcohol available on premises; BYOB allowed for members and guests

simulator_hardware.notes: Ceiling-mounted unit with radar, infrared, and high-speed imaging for real-time club and ball analytics

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs?
faq.answer: The website does not specify whether clubs are provided or if you need to bring your own. It is recommended to bring your own clubs for the best experience.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: Yes, members and guests are allowed to bring their own food and drink (BYOB). The venue also has a self-serve '19th Hole Pantry' with non-alcoholic beverages and snacks available for purchase.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not provide a specific time estimate for playing 18 holes, as it depends on the number of players, pace of play, and the specific course chosen.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is kid-friendly, offering Par-3 courses well-suited for beginners, as well as 'closest to the pin' and kid-oriented games.
